QF.id: Evaluating Fund Performance and Fund Selection_4

question.text: Which of the following best describes 'absolute return'?

answer.text: The return of a fund relative to its benchmark index
The total percentage return on an investment without any annualization
The return adjusted for inflation over a specific period
The return after deducting all fees and expenses

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Absolute return is the simple percentage gain or loss on an investment over a period, without converting to an annualized figure or comparing to any benchmark. **The Trap:** Candidates confuse absolute return with 'absolute return strategy' (hedge fund concept) or with real returns (inflation-adjusted). **Cross-Exam Context:** Understanding absolute vs annualized vs risk-adjusted returns is fundamental — exam questions often test whether candidates can distinguish between these measures. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** When clients ask 'how much did I make?', they usually want the absolute return. But for comparing across time periods, always convert to CAGR.
